---
title: "OrgPath FAQ"
subtitle: "Plain-Language Answers to the Questions Operators Actually Ask"
date: 2026-05-11
---

::: {.callout-note}
## Why this page exists

Most OrgPath confusion isn't about the canon ‚Äî the canon is precise.
It's about which canonical artifact answers which operator question,
and which terminology means what. This FAQ collects the questions
that have actually been asked during assessment and migration work,
in the order they tend to come up, with the shortest accurate answer
and one or two pointers to the deeper doc.

If your question isn't here, that's a contribution opportunity ‚Äî open
a PR adding it.
:::

## Storage and authority

### Where does OrgPath data actually live?

In three places at once, each playing a different role:

| Layer | Where | What it holds |
|-------|-------|---------------|
| **Vocabulary** | `src/uiao/canon/data/orgpath/codebook.yaml` (Git) | The valid OrgPath codes and their hierarchy |
| **Assignment** | `extensionAttribute1` on each Entra principal | The actual OrgPath value carried by each user/device |
| **Authority** | The HR system (Workday / SAP HR / HRIS) | The source of truth that drives the assignment |

See [Where OrgTree & OrgPath Data Lives](where-data-lives.qmd) for the
full mental model.

### Is OrgPath stored in a "registry"?

No ‚Äî there is no separate registry database. The codebook is a YAML
file in Git; the per-user values live on the Entra directory object
itself. The top-level `registry/` directory in this repo holds
*Governance OS adapter manifests* (pipeline sequencing, boundary
definitions). It is **not** the OrgPath store.

### Is it stored in Entra ID extension attributes? Are those attributes pre-defined?

Yes, in `extensionAttribute1`. The slot itself is **pre-defined by
Microsoft** ‚Äî `extensionAttribute1..15` exist on every Entra tenant
out of the box. UIAO doesn't create the attribute; it *claims* `extensionAttribute1`
as the canonical OrgPath slot.

The *values* in that slot are populated during migration and
maintained by HR provisioning. See
[ADR-063](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-063-orgpath-storage-slot-binding.md)
for the rationale (and why not `extensionAttribute2..15`, directory
extension schemas, or `employeeOrgData`).

### Are OrgPath codes pre-defined or built during AD assessment?

The *valid set of codes* (the codebook) is built during assessment ‚Äî
it's deliverable UIAO_151. The *Microsoft attribute slot* that
carries them is pre-existing in every tenant. So: structure is
pre-defined, vocabulary is assessment-defined, values are
migration-time + ongoing HR provisioning.

### Who decides what OrgPath value a given user has?

The HR system. The canon's operating rule is: *"HR system is the
authoritative source ‚Äî IT never manually edits OrgPath values."* Any
IT-originated write is itself a drift event under the provenance
rules in
[UIAO_163](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/UIAO_163_Drift_Detection_Engine_Specification.md).

### What if HR is wrong?

The drift engine still detects the disagreement between HR and Entra
and flags it as **Value Drift** (P2 High). When `auto_remediate=true`
the engine writes HR's value back to `extensionAttribute1`. When HR is
suspected of being stale, the finding is escalated for human review
rather than auto-applied ‚Äî see the
[Drift Detection Walk-through](drift-walkthrough.qmd) for the full
finding payload and severity ladder.

### What about contractors / non-HR-managed users?

If the principal isn't represented in the authoritative HR system,
they need an alternate source of authority documented in the
governance steward log. The most common pattern is a separate
contractor-management system that writes to a dedicated HR-equivalent
feed; the codebook itself doesn't care about the source, only that
*some* authoritative system owns the value.

## The codebook

### Where do new OrgPath codes come from?

A pull request adds a new entry to
`src/uiao/canon/data/orgpath/codebook.yaml`. The PR follows the
canonical Contributor Workflow
([UIAO_172](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/UIAO_172_Canonical_Contributor_Workflow.md))
‚Äî CI validates the regex, hierarchy, and JSON Schema; reviewers
approve; the codebook is signed and merged.

### How deep can an OrgPath go?

Up to 8 segments after the root, per
[ADR-062](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-062-orgpath-depth-extension.md).
The regex is `^ORG(-[A-Z0-9]{2,6}){0,8}$`. The original cap of 4
proved too shallow for federal-agency and military-command
hierarchies (Department ‚Üí Sub-agency ‚Üí Bureau ‚Üí Office ‚Üí Division ‚Üí
Branch ‚Üí Section ‚Üí Team).

### Are deprecated codes ever cleaned up automatically?

No. A code marked `status: deprecated` with a `replaced_by` successor
triggers **Phantom Drift** (P3) for any user still carrying it; the
engine auto-reassigns those users to the successor. The deprecated
*codebook entry itself* is retained for audit history ‚Äî never deleted
automatically ‚Äî and is removed only via a governed PR.

### What about codes with zero matching users?

Those are **Orphan Drift** (P3) ‚Äî flagged for governance review but
never auto-deleted. The codebook entry might be temporarily empty
during a reorganization, or might represent a structural placeholder
the org wants to retain. The operator decides.

## Devices, Azure resources, and service principals

### Is OrgPath stored on devices the same way as users?

Mostly. Entra-joined client devices use `device.extensionAttribute1`,
exactly like users. Azure Arc servers (and Azure resources tagged for
governance) use an **ARM tag named `OrgPath`** instead, because that's
the addressable surface for `Microsoft.HybridCompute/machines`.
See [Where Data Lives ‚Äî Devices and Azure](where-data-lives-devices-and-azure.qmd).

### What if a device is both Entra-joined and Arc-enrolled?

It carries the OrgPath in *both* slots and the canon treats string
equality between them as a required invariant. **Runtime
cross-plane enforcement is deferred** ‚Äî today, equality is achieved
via adapter-side write dispatch
([`EntraDeviceOrgPathAdapter`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/adapters/entra_device_orgpath.py)),
not by a drift-engine equality check. A future ADR will introduce
that runtime check.

### Are service principals / managed identities in scope?

Per [ADR-063](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-063-orgpath-storage-slot-binding.md),
yes ‚Äî the canonical slot is `servicePrincipal.extensionAttribute1`.
However, the drift engine v1 reads only the user and device planes;
SP consumption is deferred.

### Why not just use an Intune scope tag for devices?

Intune scope tags *derive* from `extensionAttribute1` ‚Äî they aren't
an independent storage location. Putting the OrgPath in a scope tag
directly would create two sources of truth and break the agreement
rule with the Entra device's `extensionAttribute1`. See
[Customer narrative ch. 5 ‚Äî OrgPath and Intune](../customer-documents/orgpath-narrative/05-orgpath-and-intune.qmd)
for the lifecycle.

## Drift detection

### What's the difference between the five drift categories?

| Category | What broke | Severity |
|----------|-----------|----------|
| **Schema** | Value violates regex or type | P1 |
| **Value** | Schema OK, but disagrees with HR | P2 |
| **Hierarchy** | References a parent that no longer exists | P1 |
| **Orphan** | Codebook entry with zero users | P3 |
| **Phantom** | User carrying a deprecated code | P3 |

The [Drift Walk-through](drift-walkthrough.qmd) has concrete OrgPath
examples and sample `DriftFinding` JSON for each.

### How often does the drift engine run?

The cadence is defined by the deployment, not the canon ‚Äî typical
production cadences are nightly for full population scans and
on-demand via CLI for targeted re-checks after remediation. The
engine itself is stateless between runs; every scan is a fresh
snapshot.

### Can I dry-run the drift engine?

Yes ‚Äî `DriftScanReport` carries a `dry_run` boolean. With `dry_run=true`
the engine emits findings but applies no remediation; this is the
default for new tenants and for CI gates.

## Tenant-specific concerns

### My tenant already uses `extensionAttribute1` for something else. What do I do?

Don't write over it. Follow the rebind procedure in
[ADR-063 ¬ß3](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-063-orgpath-storage-slot-binding.md):
snapshot the prior values to the Evidence Fabric, relocate the
inherited use to `extensionAttribute2` or `extensionAttribute3` via
the upstream provisioning system, verify cutover, *then* enable
OrgPath writes. Tenants where the slot cannot be vacated are blocked
from the modernization pipeline by design ‚Äî that decision sits with
the governance steward, not the deploying operator.

### Can I just pick a different slot in my tenant?

No. The slot is not a per-tenant configuration knob. Attempting to
retarget in code (e.g., editing `orgpath_plane` in a
`ComputerDisposition`) is a canon violation and fails CI under
[UIAO_172](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/UIAO_172_Canonical_Contributor_Workflow.md).

## Diagrams and rendering

### Do we have images for this?

The image *prompts* and *registry entries* are in the repo
(UIAO-FIG-012 OrgPath storage, UIAO-FIG-013 drift engine loop,
UIAO-FIG-014 device/Azure routing). The *rendered PNGs* are produced
on demand by the Gemini-based image pipeline. The status of a given
figure is recorded in
[`src/uiao/canon/image-registry.yaml`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/image-registry.yaml)
under `status:` ‚Äî `draft` means the prompt exists but the PNG hasn't
been generated yet; `current` means it's rendered and approved.

### How do I render a figure?

Two paths, both documented in the
[Image Pipeline & Registry Guide](../academy/image-pipeline-guide.qmd):

- **Local:** `export GEMINI_API_KEY=‚Ä¶` then
  `python scripts/generate_images.py`.
- **GitHub Actions:** Dispatch the `image-gen.yml` workflow with
  `commit_back: true` for canon figures so `file_sha256` is
  backfilled atomically with the PNG landing.

### How do I find if a figure I need already exists?

Use the registry search CLI:

```bash
python scripts/tools/image_registry_search.py "your topic"
```

Or filter by audience / document-type / visual-style. The CLI ranks
by weighted field match with `reuse_score` as the tiebreaker, so the
most-reused canonical image for a subject surfaces first. Prefer
`[IMAGE-REF: UIAO-FIG-NNN]` reuse over a fresh `[IMAGE-NN: ‚Ä¶]` prompt
‚Äî cheaper to render, visually consistent, drift-tracked.

## Where to go next

- **First-time reader:** [Where OrgTree & OrgPath Data Lives](where-data-lives.qmd)
- **Device / Azure operator:** [Where Data Lives ‚Äî Devices and Azure](where-data-lives-devices-and-azure.qmd)
- **Governance / drift operator:** [Drift Detection Walk-through](drift-walkthrough.qmd)
- **Codebook editor:** [OrgPath Codebook (UIAO_151)](codebook.qmd)
- **Image pipeline operator:** [Image Pipeline & Registry Guide](../academy/image-pipeline-guide.qmd)
- **Canon spec reader:** [`src/uiao/canon/UIAO_150‚Ä¶UIAO_176`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/tree/main/src/uiao/canon)
